question.text: An exporter expects to receive USD 200,000 in 3 months. To hedge using USDINR futures, the exporter should:

explanation.text: **The Logic:** An exporter will receive USD and needs to sell USD → INR. To hedge, sell USDINR futures. Number of lots = 200,000/1,000 = 200 lots. If USD weakens (USDINR falls), futures profit offsets lower spot conversion. **The Trap:** Exporters SELL futures (to lock in the selling rate); importers BUY futures (to lock in the buying rate). **Cross-Exam Context:** Hedging strategy is fundamental to Series I and VIII. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** The hedge locks in approximately the current futures rate, regardless of where spot moves by the time of actual conversion.
